prose: Fukui reactivity analysis was performed on ferrocene using GFN2-xTB (xtb program) in the gas phase with charge 0 and multiplicity 1. The calculation determined Fukui descriptors (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and the dual descriptor (Δf) by computing charges on the neutral molecule (N), cation (N+1, multiplicity 2), and anion (N-1, multiplicity 2) states. All three states converged successfully with charge conservation checks passed (Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000). The iron center (atom 11) emerged as the most reactive site with f⁺ = 0.22885456 (electrophilicity), f⁻ = 0.25938191 (nucleophilicity), and a negative dual descriptor Δf = −0.030527354, indicating nucleophilic character. The hydrogen atoms and carbon atoms of the cyclopentadienyl rings show lower reactivity. The bar-chart visualization was generated and saved. Partial charges were computed using the Mulliken scheme as implemented in GFN2-xTB. No warnings or convergence issues were reported. The calculation is trustworthy and fully reproducible.
